Over 1 Million Families Were About to Lose Their Home Movies Forever — Then They Discovered This Simple Fix

SEE TODAY'S SPECIAL OFFER

Over 25,000+ ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Reviews & Counting!

"Best thing ever. Fast, safe & secure way to convert memories we could no longer view. We couldn't believe how great it felt seeing them. We laughed and cried."

Right now, in closets and attics across America, irreplaceable family memories are slowly dying.

VHS tapes degrade roughly 10-20% per decade. Film reels become brittle and unwatchable. Old photos fade, curl, and stick together. And one house fire, one basement flood, one forgotten box in storage — and they're gone forever.

The worst part? Most families already know this is happening. They've been meaning to do something about it for years. But the thought of spending hundreds of hours scanning, recording, file transferring, and organizing feels completely overwhelming.

That's exactly why over 1 million families have turned to a company called iMemories — and it's changing the way families preserve their most precious moments.

Founded by veteran entrepreneur Mark Rukavina, iMemories is headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona with 250+ digital restoration specialists in a world-class facility dedicated to one thing: turning old home movies, film reels, and photos into crystal-clear digital files that can be watched on any modern device.

Over the past 20+ years, iMemories has become the #1 name in home media digitization — trusted by major brands like Walgreens, Best Buy, Kodak, and The UPS Store to handle their customers' digitization needs behind the scenes.

The company has been featured in The New York Times, Forbes, Good Housekeeping, Fast Company, ABC, USA Today, and FOX.

What Exactly Is iMemories?

iMemories is a full-service, mail-in digitization company that professionally converts old home movies, film reels, photo prints, slides, and negatives into high-quality digital files — so families can view, share, and stream them on any modern device.

To date, iMemories has digitized over 100 MILLION memories...

WITHOUT LOSING OR DAMAGING A SINGLE ONE.

That perfect safety record matters — because these aren't just files. They're the only recording of a grandmother's voice. A child's first steps. A wedding day that happened before smartphones existed. Losing them would be permanent.

That's why massive companies like Walgreens, Best Buy, Kodak, and The UPS Store don't do their own digitization — they send it to iMemories. If the biggest names in retail trust them with their customers' memories, families can feel confident doing the same.

How Does It Work? (It's Shockingly Simple)

The entire process with iMemories takes about 5 minutes of effort and just 1-2 weeks from start to finish. Here's how it works:

iMemories ships a crush-proof, waterproof, shock-resistant box called the SafeShip Kit straight to the door. Families just toss in their old VHS tapes, film reels, photos — whatever they have...

No sorting. No labeling. No organizing required.

Then ship it back using the pre-paid FedEx label included in the kit. iMemories' team of 250+ digital restoration specialists converts everything to modern digital format, enhanced with their proprietary McKenzie™ AI technology that upscales and restores quality while preserving the natural look.

Once digitized, the memories are instantly accessible through the iMemories streaming app (works on all devices: phones, tablets, even TVs) — like having a personal Netflix library, but filled with family home movies. They can also be downloaded, or ordered on DVD, Blu-ray, or USB thumb drive.

And every single original is returned safely. With over 1,000,000 customers and 100 million+ memories handled, iMemories has never lost or damaged a single one. Not once. Not ever.

Every item ships via FedEx in crush-proof, waterproof packaging — and once digitized, files live securely in the cloud. No more worrying about computer crashes, misplaced hard drives, or losing everything to a house fire or flood. These memories are protected forever.

What Formats Can They Convert?

iMemories handles virtually every old format — no matter how outdated:

  • Videotapes: VHS, VHS-C, Betamax, 8mm, Hi8, Video8, Digital8, MiniDV, MicroMini
  • Movie Films: 8mm, Super 8, 16mm (with and without sound)
  • Photos: Photo prints, slides, negatives

Most families don't even own a VCR anymore, let alone a projector for 8mm film or a player for Hi8 tapes. iMemories has all the equipment — families don't need any of it.

7 Reasons Families Choose iMemories

  • Perfect Safety Record — 100 million+ memories digitized and never lost or damaged a single one. Trusted by Walgreens, Best Buy, and Kodak.
  • Stream Anywhere, Anytime — the iMemories app works on Apple TV, Fire TV, Roku, iOS, Android, and 10+ other platforms. Like Netflix for family home movies.
  • Free No-Obligation Quote — get a fixed price to digitize everything before committing to a single dollar.
  • McKenzie™ AI Enhancement — proprietary technology restores and upscales old footage to look better than the day it was filmed.
  • No Effort Required — no sorting, no labeling, no organizing. iMemories' 250+ specialists handle everything from start to finish.
  • Easy Sharing With Family — send clips to relatives by text, email, or social media in seconds. Grandchildren can watch from across the country.
  • All Originals Returned Safely — every tape, reel, and photo comes back via FedEx in crush-proof packaging. Always.

As Seen In

With 25,000+ verified 5-star reviews and a 4.8 out of 5 average rating, iMemories is one of the highest-rated services in the entire digitization industry. Families everywhere are calling it "the best decision they ever made" and "worth every penny."

iMemories backs every order with a 100% Satisfaction & 30-Day Money Back Guarantee. There is genuinely zero risk — families who aren't completely satisfied get a full refund. No questions asked.

Over 1 million families have already taken action to protect their most precious memories. Every day those tapes sit in a closet, they degrade a little more. The only question is: how much longer can they afford to wait?
